observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request for write-off receivable accounts which could no longer be collected due to the liable persons were already deceased and those receivables which were dormant for more than 10 years, after all the remedies have been exhausted following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Verification will be done and request for write-off will be made.
observations.reason: The request for write-off was not given priority by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Devise means or procedures to strengthen the internal control of the LGU with regard to accounting, monitoring and reporting of the aforecited reciprocal accounts;

Be cautious and mindful in making journal entries with regard to Due to Other Funds / Due from Other Funds. Always anticipate that reciprocal accounts always have a corresponding entry in another fund and must always tally or equal in amount; and

Make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the Due to/from Other Funds accounts as well as the related receivables and income accounts in order to present fairly the actual financial condition and financial position of the Municipality as at December 31, 2020. Also, properly disclose the nature and composition of the reciprocal accounts in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office will review the previous and current year’s entries, and will prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts.


Municipal Accountant promised to analyze prior years’ entries and will make adjustments if necessary.
observations.reason:
The review of the previous years’ entries made on the reciprocal accounts was not prioritized.

Entries on the reciprocal accounts in current year shows that transactions involving these accounts were not made properly.

Analysis of the affected accounts was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Local School Board, ensure that the total share on the additional one per cent tax on real property from the previous year be completely appropriated in succeeding year’s SEF Annual Budget to ensure funding of priority programs, projects and activities of public schools in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The establishment of the LSB will be prioritized. SEF budget will be made to properly appropriate the funds for SEF.
observations.reason: In CY 2021, no LSB was functioning, thus no SEF Budget was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish and maintain GAD Database to provide accurate information and bases for an effective gender planning, programming and policy formulation, pursuant to Section 4.1, B.1 of the PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: GAD-FPS will invest in the training of their members, and will coordinate with the Municipal Civil Registry (MCR) in the establishment of the GAD Database.
observations.reason: The GAD-FPS found it difficult to establish their own database.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct a systematic and complete physical count of PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 and ensure timely submission of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor;
Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts; and
Require the Accountant to work back and review the entries in the PPE Ledgers with unreconciled balances and make the necessary adjusting entries on the affected accounts; and
Consider designating a GSO, not from any department with demanding responsibility, who will discharge on a full-time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies and property, maintain updated Property Cards per category on all Agency’s assets as recorded in the list of PPEs of accounting records, and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE.
For missing PPE items which can no longer be found/located despite great effort, we recommended that Management strictly follow the guidelines of Section 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items of government agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the required BRS, prepare subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the financial statements and other documents; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the BRS to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances of RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income. We also recommended that Management strive to further improve the process relative to the RPT and SET collections by:
Requiring the ICO-Municipal Accountant to follow the proper process in the establishment and recording of the RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions prescribed Sections 20 and 36 (1) Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs;
Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers pertaining to RPT and SET Receivables; and
Direct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their data relevant to the amount collectible for the year, and that the reconciled data be used by the ICO-Municipal Accountant to establish the RPT/SET Receivables and to properly guide Treasury Office in the collections of these receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund classification; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review. We also recommended that the BAC revisit the provisions of the 2016 Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the Procurement Law, specifically in employing different alternative modes of procurement, to ensure its compliance at all times.
We also directed the attention of Management to Section 4.0, the Penalty Clause, of COA Circular No. 2009-001, which states that the inability of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements of the same must be subject to administrative disciplinary action and the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all end-user units of the Agency to prepare the PPMP, incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for supplies, materials and PPE during the year. This must be duly evaluated and reviewed by the Municipal Budget Officer and be submitted to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation in a comprehensive, workable and realistic APP for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity (HoPE) as basis for procurement.
We also discouraged the use of other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses accounts in the Annual Budget to cater other procurements, such as the use of Other Services and other accounts to cater procurement of office supplies. This must be resorted to only in the presence of resolutions for realignment or augmentation.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all future procurements for office supplies, other supplies and PPEs be supported by an approved PPMP/APP, pursuant to the appropriate documentary requirements provided in COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant improve the internal control in his department by ensuring that: (a) the amount of claims for travels are consistent with the provisions of EO No. 77, specifically the rates and apportionment of the DTE and the allowable reimbursement for actual hotel accommodation; and (b) the supporting documents for every claim for travel are complete, appropriate and duly signed. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit all the liquidation relevant to the cash advances for travel.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Agency’s officers and employees to revisit the guidelines in claiming the DTE, consistent with the provisions of EO No. 77, to ensure compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements made by the Municipality are supported with complete documentation as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that the attached documents, such as the purchase orders, AIRs, etc. were properly signed and accomplished in compliance to the applicable laws and regulations to ascertain the validity and propriety of every transaction made by the Municipality;
Direct the BAC to revisit the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184, specially on the use of different alternative modes of procurement; and
Direct the BAC to ensure that every issuance made by the Committee must be signed by at least majority of its members; and to ensure that in case of using alternative modes of procurement, the justification for its use must be clear and relevant to the mode of procurement used.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices to immediately implement the PPAs pertinent to the unutilized portion amounting to ₱9,631,410.00 as embodied in the Municipality’s Investment Plan.
We also recommended that Management judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent EDF and ensure its full utilization for the benefit of the constituents and local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
We also gave emphasis on the provision of Section 3.2.3 of the aforementioned JMC which states that PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent EDF must be well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to promptly secure immediately the needed documents to facilitate the timely preparation of the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, pursuant to the procedures of the aforementioned Circular, to facilitate verification thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to refrain from charging non-priority expenses in the LDRRMF, instead, prioritize the procurement of equipment useful for disaster prevention, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery. We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer revisit the aforementioned guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF before charging certain expenditures against the said fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the BAC ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete and appropriate documentation and that each attached document are properly signed and accomplished.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to establish and maintain GAD database to provide accurate information and bases for an effective gender planning, programming and policy formulation and utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on RA No. 9710 Magna Carta for Women, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 and other relevant international commitments.
We also recommended Management to consider attribution of a portion or the whole of the Agency’s major programs to the GAD Budget. As provided in PCW Memorandum Circular 2020-05, agencies are advised to use the tool for budget attribution found in Section 1.2.2.2 of the same Circular.

We recommended that Management:
Fill-out the vacant positions, as prescribed in Section 443 of RA No. 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines and the rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission (CSC), for more effective administration of the offices concerned which will benefit the Municipality as a whole;
Give priority in hiring the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal GSO, because these positions are vital in the discharge of the respective mandate of the offices concerned; and
Consider effecting the personnel movement in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer and Human Resource Management (HRM) Officer to initiate the filling-up of one DRRM Staff position and to properly institutionalize the organization of LDRRMO as prescribed under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.
We also recommended that Management reconsider the designation of the two DRRM staff to other offices. In this pandemic situation, it is important that the LDRRMO is equipped with personnel solely dedicated to address problems involving disaster response and management. Considering the nature of work of the designated personnel as GSO and RCC, other officers may be assigned to these positions and not the DRRM staffs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the organization of the LSB pursuant to the aforementioned provisions and ensures that its composition is in accordance with Section 98 (b) (3) of RA 7160. We also recommended that the LSB, upon organization, ensure that an Annual SEF Budget is prepared and approved before the start of the ensuing year, following the schedule provided in Section 5.5 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
We emphasized that the balance of SEF per bank statement was ₱1,933,085.99 as of December 31, 2021, as such, we recommended that Management ensure that this be fully appropriated in the ensuing year’s SEF Budget and see to it that priority PPAs of public schools in the Municipality are properly funded.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will start the analysis of the cash in bank account and promised to give attention on the preparation of BRSs.




The preparation of subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts will be started and the updated cash books of the Municipal Treasurer will be maintained. 

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasure will meet and coordinate often to reconcile the variances.
observations.reason: The preparation of bank reconciliation statements was not prioritized by the Municipal Accountant due to the demanding responsibilities of his Office.

observations.managementAction:
Management considered deducting the cash advances of the outgoing officers from their terminal benefits.








Appropriate legal action will be considered.
observations.reason:
The remaining unliquidated balances of Cash Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Advances to Officers and Employees as of December 31, 2021 are ₱322,197.52 and ₱114,633.00, respectively.







Some of these cash advances relates to the former Municipal Treasurers that were either deceased or retired. 




This was not given priority.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions of Chapter 7, Vol 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs’ with regard to the proper accounting entry in books for procurement of supplies and equipment;

Make adjusting entries for those unconsumed supplies and unrecorded equipment that were previously recognized as expense and reclassify the same to their respective Inventory/PPE accounts as well as record the same to the Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, accordingly; and

Require the GSO to record the unrecognized Supplies and Equipment in the Stock Card and PPE Ledger Card, respectively, and to prepare the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for those unrecorded PPE and have it acknowledged by the recipient or user of the property in order to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant promised that the subsequent purchases of supplies will be recorded as inventory and not as outright expense. 

Ledger cards and stock cards will also be maintained.
observations.reason:
Adjustments were not made due to inability to conduct inventory taking of the remaining supplies and unrecorded equipment.


The maintenance of stock cards was not given priority.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the physical count of all PPEs and ensure timely submission of RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor; 

Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts; and

Require the Accountant to work back and review the entries in the PPE Subsidiary Ledgers with unreconciled balances and make the necessary adjusting entries on the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction:
A complete inventory-taking will take place in subsequent years.




Analysis of prior years’ entries on PPE accounts will be made.
observations.reason:
Partial inventory-taking was done by the GSO-Designate. Only the immovable and some high value movable PPEs were verified.


Maintenance of ledger cards and property cards was not given priority.



Review of entries relevant to PPEs was not yet given priority by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to revert accounts payable which has been outstanding for two years or more to Government Equity as prescribed under Section 98 of PD 1445 DBM–COA Joint Circular No. 99-6 dated November 13, 1999.

The Municipal Accountant must regularly monitor and prepare the aging of Payable accounts and ensure that the same is properly recorded and documented in the books as well as fully disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements in order for Management and Stakeholders to be properly informed of the nature and detailed breakdown of such accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements all the necessary details and relevant information relative to the Loans Payable pursuant to Paragraph 127 of IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to follow the proper process in recording RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions prescribed under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and Section 248 of RA No. 7160.

The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor must have proper coordination and delineation of responsibilities with respect to the submission of the required reports - the List of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer that will serve as basis in recognizing RPT and SET Receivables by the Accountant, and the Assessment Roll to be submitted by the Assessor that will serve as basis in preparing such list by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction:
The ICO-Municipal Accountant will review his working papers and prepare adjusting entries to correct the same.

Reports of the concerned officers will be reconciled, and will serve as the basis in the recognition of the tax receivables.
observations.reason:
Entries to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables were made, however, the basis used was not correct and the entries upon receipt of payment and distribution of collection were not made properly by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.

The concerned officers do coordinate but their reports are still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit the DVs/Payroll and its supporting documents to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month,

Strengthen the internal control in processing financial transactions and ensure that payment of DVs/Payroll must only be undertaken when they were duly approved, properly accomplished and supported with complete documentation, otherwise, return the same to the claimant and process only upon completion of the required documents, and

Remind the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, as amended by Section 41 of RA No. 10951, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.managementAction:
The ICO-Municipal Accountant will communicate with the different department heads to fast-track the submission of their claims/transactions to his Office, to facilitate the on-time pre-audit and recording of transactions and submission to the Office of the Auditor.

Appropriate measures will be implemented fully.
observations.reason:
Still delayed in submission of accounts in CY 2021. The delay was caused of incomplete and inappropriate documentation for some transactions.

Some suggested measures are still not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe/ comply with the timeline for the submission of Financial Reports especially the monthly Trial Balances that used to be neglected by the LGU.

Devise Accounting procedures/ methods to cope up with the submission of Accounting Reports to COA as well as other Financial Reports required by other agencies. Constantly remind/coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Offices within the LGU to submit the pending reports/ documents in order to avoid delay in the preparation and submission of financial reports to COA.
observations.managementAction:
The IOC-Municipal Accountant exerted more effort in the coordination with the different office of the Municipality in the submission of their accounts to his Office. 

Conferences will be held among department heads to discussed this deficiency.
observations.reason:
The preparation of monthly trial balances was not prioritized by the ICO-Municipal Accountant, this is due to the demanding responsibilities of his Office and the lack of personnel in his Office. 

Measures are not fully implemented due to lack of personnel and some measures are not prioritized.

observations.managementAction: The GSO has conducted the actual inventory and taking some pictures of PPE owned by LGU. The Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO is in the process for the updating of the property Ledger Card.
observations.reason: Partial inventory-taking was done. Only immovable and high-value movable PPEs were subjected to the count.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will no longer grant another cash advance in case the Accountable Officers cannot settle their previous cash advance.


Meeting with department heads will be held to discuss this concern.


Meeting with department heads will be held to discuss this concern.
observations.reason:
Negligence on the part of the Accountable Officers to liquidate on time upon return to their respective work stations.


Some suggested measures are not fully implemented.


Measures are not fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management warrant the timely submission of documents as required by the memorandum circular so as to ensure the proper and effective implementation of the programs charged under the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO raised the issue to the chief executive for hiring of additional registered social worker or even casual employees to cater the increasing demand for social services and to properly implement its programs and activities.
observations.reason: Lack of MSWDO personnel handling children cases programs and activities.

observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Mayor designated OIC- MENRO to follow up the approval of this proposed 10-year SWM Program.
observations.reason: The SWMP is still under review and for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS fast-track the establishment of a GAD database to provide accurate information and basis for an effective gender planning, programming and policy formulation pursuant to Section 4.1.B.1 of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is scheduled to start its CBMS enumeration last first quarter of 2020, however the survey was postponed indefinitely due to covid 19 pandemic.
observations.reason: CBMS is on- going for the formulization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU formulate rules and regulations which will serve as their own criteria for the Implementation of the AICS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the MSWDO- designated to present the draft AICS resolution to the legislative office for deliberation and approval.
observations.reason: AICS draft guidelines is in the process for the finalization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDC to prepare and submit on time the above-mentioned report to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation on the Agency’s utilization of the said Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Honorable Mayor called the attention of the concerned personnel tasked for the preparation of the required reports and to submit the same in a prescribed format.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination between the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer for the status of utilization and accomplishment of 20% development projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management require both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to promptly cause the timely remittance of liabilities due to the national and corporate government agencies in order not to compromise the grant of benefits to the LGU’s officials and staff and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments and to warrant timely use of these resources by the government agencies concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant was directed by the Municipal Mayor to prepare necessary documents earlier to avoid the possible delay of all remittances.
observations.reason: Negligence to prepare earlier the documents related for the remittance of inter- agency payables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Auditors the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We also reminded Management and the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (3) of PD No. 1445, and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer’s office set a schedule for the submission of accounts every 5th day of the succeeding month.
observations.reason: Delayed transmittal of accounts by the Municipal Treasurers Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all department heads concerned to exert coordinated efforts in order to come up with an updated RPCPPE.

We further recommended that the Mayor direct all Local Government Unit (LGU) officials/employees having direct concerns over these unrecorded properties be directed to retrieve the necessary documents and submit the same to the Property Officer and Accounting Unit for proper recording/accounting in the agency’s books and that similar transactions in the future be duly accounted for upon acceptance.

See relevant findings in the current year.



See relevant findings in the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all Accountable Officer concerned be required to liquidate their cash advances on time so that proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation on the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant, in his capacity as the internal auditor, must strictly implement the rules and regulations on cash advances to avoid the accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances.
	
Lastly, restrain from granting cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was reminded by the current Mayor on this regard.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated claims for advance travel. We noted improvement on the liquidation of regular cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all concerned be required to observe full compliance to the aforecited rules and regulations to warrant timely rectification of defects and thereby reducing the risks of government funds wastage.
observations.managementAction: A new directive will be issued in this regard and shall be strictly followed.
observations.reason: Delayed submission, succeeding contracts and purchase orders are still not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit a duly approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in order to properly address the need to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO was directed by the Municipal Mayor to follow up the approval of submitted Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: A Solid Waste Management Plan was already furnished the Audit Team but still awaiting for its approval by the DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert its best effort to implement all programmed projects under the 20 per cent development fund during the year to ensure that its objectives of desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes will be achieved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Strictly observe the rules and regulation relative to the preparation of the
monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to COA within
the prescribed period;
b) Regularly obtain copies of Bank Statements from the bank, or make
continuous reminder or follow up with the bank if the same are not obtained.
If possible, enrol in the online access/services of DBP in order to obtain soft
copies of monthly bank statements for all the funds of the LGU; and
c) Prepare/ update the Subsidiary Ledger Cards per bank accounts for all the
funds.
Further, regular coordination between the Municipal Accountant and
Treasurer must be made in order that reconciling cash balances in the respective
books and records will be adjusted timely and accordingly.

We recommended that Management:
a) Strictly comply with the laws, rules and regulation relative to the granting,
liquidation and monitoring of cash advances.
b) Require the Accountant to devise means or procedure to strengthen the
internal control with respect to granting and monitoring of cash advances in
order that the data and documents of previous cash advances will not be lost.
Ensure that the Cash Advance of retiring, resigning or transferring employee
is first settled before approving the clearance from money and property
accountability;
c) Refrain from granting additional cash advances if the previous cash advance
has not been fully settled or liquidated;
d) Send demand letters to erring Accountable Officers and Employees requiring
them to immediately settle and/or refund the remaining cash advance,
otherwise, withhold the salaries of those who neglectfully or intentionally fail
to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after demand;
e) Evaluate individually the probability of collection of outstanding cash
advances especially those Dormant Accounts and send request for write off to
COA in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
f) Enforce appropriate legal action in order to recover/ settle the unliquidated
Cash Advances of liable persons and employees and set as an example to those
erring officials and employees that their negligence or failure to liquidate cash
advances within the prescribed period has a corresponding adverse
consequence.

We recommended that Management request for write-off receivable accounts
which could no longer be collected due to the liable persons were already deceased
and those receivables which were dormant for more than 10 years, after all the
remedies have been exhausted following the procedures set forth in COA Circular
No. 2016-005.

a) Direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions
of Chapter 7, Vol 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs’ with regard to the
proper Accounting entry in books for procurement of supplies and
equipment;
b) Make adjusting entries for those unconsumed supplies and unrecorded
equipment that were previously recognized as expense and reclassify the
same to their respective Inventory/PPE accounts as well as record the same
to the Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, accordingly; and
c) Require the GSO to record the unrecognized Supplies and Equipment in the
Stock Card and PPE Ledger Card, respectively, and to prepare the
corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for those
unrecorded PPE and have it acknowledged by the recipient or user of the
property in order to establish accountability.

We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the physical
count of all PPEs and ensure timely submission of RPCPPE to the Office of
the Auditor;
b) Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated
Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, per category on all
PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with
each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE
accounts; and
c) Require the Accountant to work back and review the entries in the PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers with unreconciled balances and make the necessary
adjusting entries on the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to revert accounts
payable which has been outstanding for two years or more to Government Equity as
prescribed under Section 98 of PD 1445 DBM–COA Joint Circular No. 99-6 dated
November 13, 1999.
The Accountant must regularly monitor and prepare the aging of Payable
accounts and ensure that the same is properly recorded and documented in the books
as well as fully disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements in order for
Management and Stakeholders to be properly informed of the nature and detailed
breakdown of such accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements all the necessary details and relevant
information relative to the Loans Payable pursuant to Paragraph 127 of IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to follow the
proper process in recording RPT and SET Receivables pursuant to the provisions
prescribed under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and Section
248 of RA No. 7160.
The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor must
have proper coordination and delineation of responsibilities with respect to the
submission of the required reports- the List of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer that will serve as
basis in recognizing RPT and SET Receivables by the Accountant, and the
Assessment Roll to be submitted by the Assessor that will serve as basis in preparing
such list by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Devise mean s or procedures to strengthen the internal control of the LGU
with regard to accounting, monitoring and reporting of the aforecited
reciprocal accounts;
b) Be cautious and mindful in making journal entries with regard to Due to
Other Funds / Due from Other Funds. Always anticipate that reciprocal
accounts always have a corresponding entry in another fund and must
always tally or equal in amount; and
c) Make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to
present the correct balances of the Due to/from Other Funds accounts as well
as the related receivables and income accounts in order to present fairly the
actual financial condition and financial position of the Municipality as at
December 31, 2020. Also, properly disclose the nature and composition of the
reciprocal accounts in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Local School
Board, ensure that the total share on the additional one per cent tax on real property
from the previous year be completely appropriated in succeeding year’s SEF Annual
Budget to ensure funding of priority programs, projects and activities of public
schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 requiring the
Municipal Accountant to submit the DVs/Payroll and its supporting
documents to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month,
b) Strengthen the internal control in processing financial transactions and
ensure that payment of DVs/Payroll must only be undertaken when they
were duly approved, properly accomplished and supported with complete
documentation, otherwise, return the same to the claimant and process only
upon completion of the required documents, and
c) Remind the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or
criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and Article 218 of
the Revised Penal Code, as amended by Section 41 of RA No. 10951, which
may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render
accounts without justifiable cause.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe/ comply with
the timeline for the submission of Financial Reports especially the monthly Trial
Balances that used to be neglected by the LGU.
Devise Accounting procedures/ methods to cope up with the submission of
Accounting Reports to COA as well as other Financial Reports required by other
agencies. Constantly remind/coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and other
concerned Offices within the LGU to submit the pending reports/ documents in order
to avoid delay in the preparation and submission of financial reports to COA.

We recommended that Management establish and maintain GAD Database to
provide accurate information and bases for an effective gender planning,
programming and policy formulation, pursuant to Section 4.1, B.1 of the PCW,
DILG, DBM, and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

a) Adhere strictly to the pertinent laws, rules and regulations with regard to
current procurement of the LGU. In case of Emergency Procurement under
the Bayanihan Act, the LGU must comply with the latest procedure and
documentation required in RA No. 11469, GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 and
COA Circular 2012-001;
b) Direct the Accountant to strengthen its internal control in the pre audit of
financial transactions. Ensure that the supporting documents were complete
and properly accomplished/filled out as to details and necessary information;
otherwise, refrain from processing the claim or payment of the same; and
c) Submit the lacking documents and rectify the deficiencies of the above-
mentioned financial transactions in order to avoid suspension/ and or
disallowance in audit.

1) We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct all department heads concerned to exert coordinated efforts in order to come up with an updated RPCPPE.
2) We further recommended that all LGU officials/employees having direct concerns over these unbooked properties be directed to retrieve the necessary documents and submit the same to the Property Officer and Accounting Unit for proper recording/accounting in the Agency’s books and that similar transactions in the future be duly accounted for upon acceptance.
3) Likewise, apply the necessary depreciation costs where warranted.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU issued an executive order for the composition of inventory committee. Meanwhile, the GSO has conducted the actual inventory and taking some pictures Inventory of PPE owned by LGU.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO is in the process for the updating of the property Ledger Card.
observations.reason: Absence of Inventory Committee

observations.recommendation:
4) We reiterated our recommendation that all the Accountable Officers concerned be required to liquidate their cash advances on time so that their proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation on the part of the financial statements.
5) The Municipal Accountant in hi capacity as the internal auditor must strictly implement the rules and regulations on cash advances to avoid the accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances.
6) Lastly, refrain from granting cash advances to AOs, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant will no longer grant another cash advance in case the Accountable Officers cannot settle their previous cash advance.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant was reminded by the current Mayor on this regard.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant together with the GSO is in the process for the updating of the property Ledger Card.
observations.reason: Negligence on the part of the Accountable Officers to liquidate on time upon return to their respective work stations.

observations.recommendation: 7) We recommended the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE to a Special Trust Fund Account pursuant to the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 to ensure that the unspent balances are readily available for relief and recovery projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Upon received of the Audit Observation memorandum, the Office of the Municipal Accountant immediately prepared the documents for the transfer of unexpended Municipal LDRRMF for Quick Response and MOOE to the LGU MDRRMF trust fund account, annually.
observations.reason: Negligence on the part of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: 8) We reiterated our recommendation that Management warrant the timely submission of documents as required by the memorandum circular so as to ensure the proper and effective implementation of the programs charged under the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: Lack of personnel handling the children cases. The MSWDO raised the issue to the chief executive for hiring of additional registered social worker or even casual employees to cater the increasing demand for social services and to properly implement its programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: 9) We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality submit a duly approved 10-year SWMP in order to properly address the need to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) designated OIC-MENRO to follow up the approval of this proposed 10- year SWM Program.
observations.reason: The MENRO suffered a coronary stroke.

observations.recommendation: 10) We recommended that the GFPS fast-track the establishment of a GAD database to provide accurate information and basis for an effective gender planning, programming and policy formulation pursuant to Section 4.1.B.1 of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA
observations.managementAction: CBMS is on- going for the formulization. The LGU is scheduled to start its CBMS enumeration last first quarter of 2020, however the survey was postponed indefinitely due to covid 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that the LGU formulate rules and regulations which will serve as their own criteria for the Implementation of the AICS.
observations.managementAction: AICS draft guidelines is in the process for the finalization. The Municipal Mayor directed the MSWDO-designated to present the draft AICS resolution to the legislative office for deliberation and approval.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that Management direct the MPDC to prepare and submit on time the above-mentioned report to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation on the Agency’s accomplishment of the said Development Fund 20% development projects.
observations.managementAction: Non submission of coordination between the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer for the status of utilization and submit the same in a prescribed format. The Honorable Mayor called the attention of the concerned personnel tasked for the preparation of the required reports and to submit the same in a prescribed format.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination

observations.recommendation: 13. We reiterated our recommendation that Management require both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to promptly cause the timely remittance of liabilities due to the national and corporate government agencies in order not to compromise the grant of benefits to the LGU’s officials and staff and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments and to warrant timely use of these resources by the government agencies concerned.
observations.managementAction: Negligence to prepare earlier the documents related for the remittance of inter- agency payables. The Office of the Municipal Accountant was directed by the Municipal Mayor to prepare necessary documents earlier to avoid the possible delay of all remittances.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Auditors the required financial transaction documents on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for prompt verification purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.
15. We also reminded Management and the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (3) of PD No. 1445, and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause
observations.managementAction: Delayed transmittal of accounts by the Municipal Accountant together with the Treasurers Office. The office of the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer’s office set a schedule for the submission of accounts every 5th day of the succeeding month.

observations.recommendation:
16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all department heads concerned to exert coordinated efforts in order to come up with an updated RPCPPE.
17. We further recommended that the Mayor direct all Local Government Unit (LGU) officials/employees having direct concerns over these unbooked properties be directed to retrieve the necessary documents and submit the same to the Property Officer and Accounting Unit for proper recording/accounting in the agency’s books and that similar transactions in the future be duly accounted for upon acceptance.
observations.managementAction:
Change in administration. The LGU issued an executive order for the composition of inventory committee. Meanwhile, the GSO has conducted the actual inventory and taking some pictures of PPE owned by LGU.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the GSO is in the process of updating its property Ledger Card.

observations.recommendation:
18. We recommended that all Accountable Officer concerned be required to liquidate their cash advances on time so that proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation on the financial statements.
19. The Municipal Accountant, in his capacity as the internal auditor, must strictly implement the rules and regulations on cash advances to avoid the accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances.
20. Lastly, restrain from granting cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: There were still unliquidated claims for advance travel. The Municipal Accountant was reminded by the current Mayor on this regard. We noted improvement on the liquidation of regular cash advances.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended that all concerned be required to observe full compliance to the aforecited rules and regulations to warrant timely rectification of defects and thereby reducing the risks of government funds wastage.
observations.managementAction: Delayed submission. A new directive will be issued in this regard and shall be strictly followed.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommended that the Municipality submit a duly approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in order to properly address the need to ensure sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: A Solid Waste Management Plan was already furnished the Audit Team but still submitted awaiting for its approval by the DENR. The MENRO was directed by the Municipal Mayor to follow up the status of Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommended that Management exert its best effort to implement all programmed projects under the 20 per cent development fund during the year to ensure that its objectives of desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes will be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Due to unforeseen events, some unimplemented programs were not implemented. The LGU reflects its unimplemented projects under Local Development Fund in the continuing appropriation and implement the same on CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
24. We recommended that all LGU officials/employees having direct concerns over these properties be directed to retrieve the necessary documents and submit the same to the General Services Officer and Accounting Unit for proper recording in the agency’s books.
25. It is further recommended that similar transactions in the future be accounted for upon acceptance.
observations.managementAction: Inability to organize the Inventory Committee. See related findings.

observations.recommendation:
26. We recommended that all department heads concerned be required to observe full compliance to the aforecited rules and regulations to warrant timely rectification of defects and thereby reduce the risks of government funds wastage and to aid the LGU Senior Management in its decision making in future transaction.
27. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require copy of request for final inspection, duly received by the COA Audit Team, in all claims for final payments for completed infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
Very late submission and incomplete. The Municipal Mayor ordered both the BAC members and the Secretariat to strictly adhere, comply and update the timely submission of documents.
The Municipal Accountant was ordered to require copy of request for final inspection, duly received by the COA Audit Team, in all claims for final payments for completed infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommended that Management direct the LDRRMO to submit or cause to be submitted on time a copy of the fund utilization report along with LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: Delayed submission. The LDRRMO submitted the LDRRM Fund Utilization Report for the quarter ending March 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: 29. We recommended that the GAD Focal Person be aware of the yearly appropriation for GAD so as to maximize and fully implement the activities related to it.
observations.managementAction: See related findings. The Municipal Mayor and the GAD focal person finalized the GAD Plan to see to it that all programs shall be accordingly utilized as planned and any programmed activities which are no longer feasible shall be reprogrammed to other GAD activities in order to address other GAD related issues.

observations.recommendation:
30. Implement immediately the PPA’s as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people; and
31. Require the Local Development Council to include in their AIPs development projects which are feasible and to assure their implementation as to target dates.
observations.managementAction: See related findings. The LGU implemented the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) funded out of 20% DF for CY 2016 and 2017 on CY 2018.

It is further recommended that similar transactions in the future be accounted for upon acceptance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Agency’s compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of submitting updated RPCPPE and the regular conduct of Physical Inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: A new OIC- Municipal Treasurer was appointed. With this, the Inventory Committee will be reactivated this CY 2018.
observations.reason: Both the GSO and the Municipal Treasurer retired from Office. No replacement yet during the year.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that all disbursements coming from the GAD/LDRRM/20% Development Funds shall be subject to the approval of the respective program managers to make sure that these disbursements are proper and form part of the approved plans/programs.
observations.managementAction: The Honorable Mayor will issue requiring all transactions for GAD/ LDRRMF/ and 20% Development Fund to be subject to the approval of the respective fund managers.
observations.reason: The senior management was unable to adopt the recommended measures due to the changes in the personnel department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to ensure that the required financial reports and accounts together with its supporting documents are submitted complete and within prescribed period.

Installation of systematic procedures governing preparation, submission and transmittal of these accounts and reports may be adopted to assure full compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant was directed by the new Municipal Mayor to observe compliance through updated submission of financial reports.
observations.reason: The position of Municipal Accountant is still vacant, since the death of the previous appointee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended full compliance by LGU-Mandaon to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003, particularly to the role of the LGU in the nationwide campaign for an effective ecological solid waste management program.

Install an effective collection and disposal of solid wastes, giving emphasis to its segregation and proper disposal.
observations.managementAction: A sanitary landfill compliant to the DENR requirements was already identified. Negotiations as to its purchase is being undertaken.
observations.reason: The LGU has a functional solid waste management program except for a sanitary landfill. Procurement of which are being planned subject to approval of site by the DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU senior Management observe prudence and utilize in full all the funds available to ensure efficient and effective delivery of primary health care benefits.
observations.managementAction: Use of the PCF shall be made priority by the LGU in the procurement of necessary medicine and medical supplies/ equipment.
observations.reason: Unutilized PCF, although reduced, was still noted.

See to it that only five percent (5%) of the agency’s funds from regular sources shall be appropriated for the current year’s LDRRM Fund to avoid confusion.

observations.recommendation: In order to pre-empt future incurrence of similar questionable claims and considering that the fiscal responsibility of the local government unit rests directly with the head of the agency, or with the official having direct supervision and control over the public funds, we recommended that every local official and employees cease to claim reimbursement of travel expenses unless purpose is specifically provided.
We recommended that management ensure the timely remittance of the premium contributions within the prescribed period in accordance with RA 9679.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact and regularly all collections turned over to him to the agency’s authorized depository bank pursuant to the foregoing regulations; and

b)	Strictly monitor the collections and turn-over activities of all accountable officers and see to it that the laws, rules and regulations governing collections and deposits are strictly complied with to ensure that the agency’s cash resources are adequately safeguarded.

a)	Implement immediately the PPA’s as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people; and

observations.managementAction: Use of the PCF shall be made priority by the LGU in the procurement of necessary medicine and medical supplies/ equipment.
observations.reason: Unutilized PCF, although reduced, was still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all its depository accounts within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements and to immediately make the necessary correction of errors and adjustments to come up with the reliable data pertaining to Cash in Bank account balance.

Moreover the Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer should regularly reconcile their accounting records and cashbook balance respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will look into the possibility and availability of funds for additional manpower for the accounting unit.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant reported to have prepared bank reconciliation statements for some accounts although they were not submitted to the auditor for verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all the accountable officer concerned be required to liquidate their cash advances on time so that proper accounting could be made thereof to ensure fairness of accounts presentation on the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant in her capacity as the internal auditor should strictly implement the rules and regulations on cash advances to avoid the accumulation of huge amount of unliquidated cash advances.

Lastly, restrain from granting of cash advances to accountable officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: The Honorable Mayor will issue a directive to require refunds or immediate settlement of these unliquidated claims for advance travels.
observations.reason: A balance of ₱424,867.61 was still unliquidated as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other personnel to ensure that the required financial reports and accounts together with its supporting documents are submitted complete and within the prescribed period.

Installation of systematic procedures governing preparation, submission and transmittal of these accounts and reports may be adopted to assure full compliance thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended agency’s compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of submitting updated RPCPPE and the regular conduct of Physical Inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: A new OIC-Municipal Treasurer was appointed. With this, the Inventory Committee will be reactivated this CY 2018.
observations.reason: Both the GSO and the Municipal Treasurer retired. No replacement yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all disbursements coming from the GAD/LDRRM/20% Development Funds shall be subject to the approval of the respective program managers to make sure that these disbursements are proper and form part of the approved plans/programs.
observations.managementAction: The Honorable Mayor will issue requiring all transactions for GAD/LDRRMF/ and 20% Development Fund to be subject to the approval of the respective department fund managers.
observations.reason: The senior management was unable to adopt the recommended measures due to the changes in the personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to ensure that the required financial reports and accounts together with its supporting documents _are submitted _complete _and within prescribed period. Installation of systematic procedures governing preparation, submission and transmittal of these accounts and reports may be adopted to assure full compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The position of Municipal Accountant is still vacant, since the death of the previous appointee. The OIC-Municipal Accountant was directed by the Municipal Mayor to observe compliance through updated submission of financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated management'’s compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of submitting updated RPCPPE and the regular conduct of Physical Inventory taking.

We recommended that the LDRRMO of LGU - Mandaon be put to task of submitting on time the required LDRRMF' Investment Plan as well as the submission of the Monthly Report on Source and Utilization. See to it that only five percent (5%) of the agency’s funds from regular sources shall be appropriated for the current year's LDRRM Fund to avoid confusion.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO readily admitted his fault but manifested that the required report was submitted before our exit conference, but only after the issuance of our Audit Observation Memorandum and a follow-up letter to the Honorable Mayor. During the same exit conference, the LDRRMO submitted his LDRRM Fund Utilization Report for the quarter ending March 31, 2018 as a sign of his commitment to regular submission of the same this year and henceforth.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO failed to submit its LDRRMF as well as the required quarterly LDRRM Fund Utilization Reports regular submission of said periodic report.

observations.recommendation: The Senior LGU management is being reminded to direct all LGU officers/employees concerned to fully comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations concerning contract reviews and/or inspection of infrastructure projects to pre-empt incurrence of unnecessary loss of government funds/resources due to defective or deficient implementation of infrastructure projects. Priority attention shall be given to infrastructure projects funded out of the National Government Agencies.
observations.managementAction: The LGU failed to submit on time the required documents despite constant reminders. Compliance hereto by the personnel concerned was directed by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: Despite constant reminders the LGU failed to submit on time the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that effective strong internal control over financial accounts/statements reporting shall be installed providing appropriate legal or administrative sanctions towards erring officers/employees.

It is further recommended that similar transactions in the future be accounted
for upon acceptance.

We recommended that all purchases of supplies and materials be properly accounted for under the appropriate Inventory Accounts. Its utilization should be reported using the SSML Use of the Annual Procurement Plan as required under the RA 9184 be strictly complied with.

We recommended that senior LGU management strictly comply with and adhere to the provisions governing grant and liquidation of cash advances in order to prevent unnecessary accumulation of cash on hand thereby mitigating the risk of fund misuse/misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer was put to task to comply and adhere to the auditing rules and regulations governing grant and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials concerned be required to fully comply with the aforecited provisions so as to enable the public to be informed of the LGU's operations.

We recommended that Management:

a. Maintain updated Property Cards per category on all property, plant, and equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant, and Equipment of accounting records, and thereafter as the transaction occurs.

b. Reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the Prepared RCPPE agreed with the supply records and the book balances in the accounting records, and vice versa.

c Maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every three years for the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE or based on the old Memorandum Receipts on File.

d. Submit the copy of the Invoice Receipt showing the certification of the Outgoing Official pertaining to the returnedturned over properties and acknowledgment receipt of the Incoming Official/s of the property surrendered by the Outgoing Official including the signatures of the witnesses for the turn-over.

We recommended full compliance by LGU-Mandaon to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003, particularly to the role of the LGU in the nationwide campaign for an effective ecological solid waste management program. Install an effective collection and disposal of solid wastes, giving emphasis to its segregation and proper disposal.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has a functional solid waste management program except for a sanitary landfill. A sanitary landfill compliant to the DENR requirements was already identified. Negotiations as to its purchase is being planned subject to approval of site by the DENR.
observations.reason: Procurement of purchase is being undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU senior management observe prudence and utilize in full all the funds available to ensure efficient and effective delivery of primary health care benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the senior management of LGU — Mandaon should extensively comply with all the provisions and guidelines enumerated in the Joint Memorandum Circular of the PCW/DILG/NEDA and DBM concerning the preparation/submission of GAD Plan and Budget as well as the proper utilization of the GAD Fund in order to address all the prevailing GAD related issues.

We recommended the following courses of action in order to rectify the aforementioned deficiencies, to wit;

1. Direct all collecting clerks to stop payment of vouchers and cash vales out of their collections.

2. Establish a Petty Cash Fund System to address the problem in petty cash expenses. A petty cash custodian may be designated.

3. Turn-over of collections must be required weekly. Thorough review of the RCDs and RAAFs must be undertaken.

4. Deposit all collections intact to prevent incurrence of undeposited collections at any given time.
observations.managementAction: While we noted significant improvement in the controls over collections, the Municipal Treasurer was still directed to continue and/ or improve management of revenue collections.
observations.reason: Some collections remained in the custody of the collecting clerk.
